core.label: Bash Fundamentals
core.description: Shell basics, commands, variables, and scripting.

topics.label: Shell & Terminal Basics
topics.description: Understand the terminal, shell types (bash, zsh, sh), and how to run commands.

topics.label: Variables
topics.description: Assign with NAME=value (no spaces), access with $NAME or ${NAME}. Variables are untyped strings.

topics.label: Quoting ('', "", $())
topics.description: Single quotes (literal), double quotes (expand variables), backticks/$(command substitution).

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/elif/else/fi, case/esac, for, while, until. Test conditions with [[ ]] or [ ].

topics.label: Functions
topics.description: Define with function name() { } or name() { }. Functions are the primary code reuse mechanism.

topics.label: Arguments ($1, $@, $#)
topics.description: Access script and function arguments with positional parameters. $0 is the script name.

topics.label: Pipes & Redirection
topics.description: | for piping, >, >>, <, 2>&1, /dev/null. Pipes connect command outputs to inputs.

topics.label: Exit Codes & set -e
topics.description: $? for last exit code, set -euo pipefail for strict mode. Always use strict mode in scripts.

topics.label: Arrays
topics.description: Indexed arrays (arr=(a b c)) and associative arrays (declare -A). Loop with ${arr[@]}.

core.label: Advanced Scripting
core.description: Text processing, process management, and best practices.

topics.label: grep, sed, awk
topics.description: Text searching (grep), stream editing (sed), and pattern processing (awk). The Unix text processing trinity.

topics.label: find & xargs
topics.description: Search for files and execute commands on results. Essential for batch operations.

topics.label: Process Management
topics.description: Background jobs (&), fg, bg, jobs, wait, kill, trap. Manage processes from scripts.

topics.label: Trap & Signal Handling
topics.description: Catch signals (SIGINT, SIGTERM) and cleanup with trap. Essential for robust scripts.

topics.label: Here Documents
topics.description: Multi-line input with <<EOF. Used for embedding config, SQL, and multi-line strings.

topics.label: cron & Scheduling
topics.description: Schedule recurring tasks with crontab. Automate backups, reports, and maintenance.

topics.label: Debugging (set -x, shellcheck)
topics.description: Trace execution with set -x and lint with ShellCheck. ShellCheck catches common bash mistakes.

topics.label: Best Practices
topics.description: Strict mode, quoting, portability, and script structure. Write reliable, maintainable shell scripts.
